WOD_2__COMMENTS__C: Customer called and was not getting his 24 volts to pump kill circuit.  New customer and just did ICON panel conversion on 2/6/25 (ICON Link #293644).  Being basically brand new, we assumed nothing issue wise with the board and on the pump kill side.  Customer is new and 60 miles from us.  Found a bad spot on board that wasn't allowing board to close the switch.  Found nothing power issue wise at all.  Replaced board and everything fired fine.  Even had pump kill circuit fused and fuse was not blown.  Tech had to come all the way back to Wray (60 miles) to get a board and go back and fix.  6 hours out of his Saturday to customer going.  This is reason for extra trip and a lot more time.  My cost is $50/hr in this tech, and trying to cover his time and mileage, with no power issue we could find that would cause board to go bad.

WOD_2__COMMENTS__C: On June 19th installed new booster pump (0994086) and motor (0992805).  Called back out on July 1 of end gun not working. NAME: CA177106
WOD_2__COMMENTS__C: Seeking special approval for this warranty due to not knowing it was an issue until machine almost crashed.  One corner motor failed and other one continued to walk into a side load and folded pipe and almost put machine to ground.  Neither of the corner overloads tripped.  This goes back to a previous Valley service bulletin about VFLEX machines that were sent out years ago with over sized heaters on the overloads for the motors.  In this case this machine had w-43 heaters and after talking with valley service they recommended that it needed w-39 heaters.  Not looking to warranty the 6- 0314753 heaters but be re-imbursed for the 6- 0314349 w-39 heaters we swapped out.

Serial # 12201595_x000D_
Came across this meter and noticed it saying Batt End. Guessing that means battery near end of life. This meter was wired so it had 12VDC supply voltage. Also looking at the ACFT reading, seems like this meter has not worked for a while. Since this meter is wired to the pivot panel. I looked up historical readings and seen it hasn't clocked a flow this water year. Last flow reading I seen on it was from 8/31/24. When swapping out with a new meter today. I noticed one of the three metal pins sticking out of the bottom missing with signs of corrosion around where pin should be.

Due to the location of this pivot and being a 4 hour round trip drive time I will be requesting extra drive time on top of the labor.  Also, before we made the trip down to diagnose the pivot, because it was a new system, the electrician that installed the roto phase and wired power into pivot was requested to go troubleshoot all the work on his end. Electricians spent 6 hours at a rate of  125 and hour going through everything and monitoring amps and power readings and found no issues and were able to isolate it to something on the pivot. NAME: CA179829
WOD_2__COMMENTS__C:
failed AgSense number 260072_x000D_
_x000D_
Icon Link installed with icon upgrade 11/9/25 ex dealer stock. Agsense unit stopped responding as on site 15/12/25, received and responded to a stop command in the morning then a few hours later would not respond or acknowledge any commands and appeared offline, inspection showed the unit was powered however the modem LED was not on or flashing, 12.9V dc was supplied and the battery voltage was measured at 4.18V , A BAC was installed, reset the device multiple times (cascade LEDs) Proc would flash and 5v led was on solid but the modem light would not even flash. Had a TUK WW on hand so swapped the modem and repeated the above checks, no change, installed the original modem and left powered up for several days, has never come live again and the client is keen to get working ASAP, suspect ICON link board has failed number260072 . Shipping replacement ex dealer stock 292907

Audrain Irrigation is submitting a warranty claim for a Valley ICONX GPS head unit sold to Frank Martin as part of two ICONX upgrade systems with GPS (Invoice 15043295)._x000D_
_x000D_
The systems were installed in May 2023, and the customer paid in May 2023. Prior to September 2023, the customer reported the pivot would intermittently stop, appearing to lose GPS communication with the ICONX panel._x000D_
_x000D_
I inspected the system approximately 15¿20 days after the initial report while on the phone with Valley Technical Support. Software was confirmed to be up to date, and the issue did not appear to be software-related. Valley advised that if the issue reoccurred, the GPS head unit should be submitted for warranty._x000D_
_x000D_
The customer contacted me again 2¿3 weeks ago reporting the same intermittent failure, with loss of GPS communication preventing proper pivot movement. Based on repeat failure and Valley guidance, the GPS head unit is being submitted for warranty replacement.

NAME: CA180761
WOD_2__COMMENTS__C: The safety circuit was mis wired in the sdu box on this machine. This was found during start up. After this was corrected the OVB was then found to be no good in a rather odd way. It is discoverable and programmable as you would expect. But it will not receive gps messaging from the rover antenna. New antenna was tried, new cable was tried, alternate RJ plugs were tried, It will not report any info from a gps antenna at all. Replaced with new one and using the antenna and cable that came with the machine it works as expected. Pictures of the wiring will be sent to Katie via email. The SDU box did not need to be replaced but factory side wiring was changed in the field. Upon receiving the OVB you will see the wifi antenna is a little damaged, it was received that way but does not seem to have an affect on the function of the unit overall.
WOD_2__APPROVAL_COMMENTS__C: Pending Part Return - KC 3/20/26 Parts Received - OK - KC 4/9/26

NAME: CA180763
WOD_2__COMMENTS__C: Pictures will be sent to Katie along with some others for a previous claim. But in this case the corner would not move and the outside tire would dig itself a hole while trying to drag the corner along if you let it run. The cause is the inside corner wheel not running due to a missing coupler half. In other words the three jaw coupler connection had one coupler on the side closest to the wheel gearbox, as well as the bronze spider was there, but the other coupler on the motor side along with the key stock was never installed, allowing the motor to spin freely but not engage the wheel gearbox. New coupler and key stock installed, working as desired. This was found during initial start up.
WOD_2__DESCRIPTION__C: IRR MS 3 JAW CPLG-BOSTON GEAR BOX
